description_long: This house features a two-story structure with a green wooden exterior and a prominent gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front facade is characterized by a wide covered porch supported by columns, with two white shuttered windows and a central entryway beneath a small gabled overhang. The side views reveal the vertical wood paneling and the extension of the porch roof, while the rear of the house is simpler, with fewer windows and a small covered area. The roofline is steep and symmetrical, with the main gable running the length of the building and smaller gables above the front windows. The house sits on a raised foundation, and the porch includes simple railings and steps leading up to the entry. Overall, the design suggests a traditional, early 20th-century American residential style.
description: A two-story green wooden house with a steep gable roof, wide front porch, and white shuttered windows, featuring traditional architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide covered porch, two white shuttered windows, central entryway, and symmetrical gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical wood paneling, the extension of the porch roof, and the end of the main gable.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain green facade, minimal windows, and a small covered area on one side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the end of the house, small windows, and the continuation of the porch structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with a small gabled overhang, wide covered porch, columns, and steps leading up to the door.
